summary: Merged prior `ifstatus` decompilation progress from #369 by moving `asm/melee/if/ifstatus.s` into `src/melee/if/ifstatus.c`, adding a new `ifstatus.h`, splitting remaining data into `asm/melee/if/ifstatus_data.s`, and updating `obj_files.mk` to build both the C object and data object. The PR body explicitly notes that data was not migrated to C yet and that only one completed function, `func_802F6194`, still required `NON_MATCHING`.
what_changed: `asm/melee/if/ifstatus.s` was renamed into `src/melee/if/ifstatus.c` and converted into a mixed C/inline-assembly translation unit.
A simple matched C function was introduced for `func_802F4910`, returning `&lbl_804A10C8` as a `HudIndex*`.
Many still-unmatched routines were preserved as CodeWarrior-style inline asm functions using `asm`, `nofralloc`, labels, and `#pragma push`/`#pragma pop` wrappers.
`func_802F6194` gained a C implementation under `#ifdef NON_MATCHING` plus an asm fallback, with a decomp.me scratch link in the diff.

All data for this slice remained in a new assembly file, `asm/melee/if/ifstatus_data.s`, including `.data`, `.sdata`, `.bss`, `.sbss`, and `.sdata2` labels such as `lbl_803F9598`, `lbl_803F9628`, `lbl_804A10C8`, and many `lbl_804DDAxx` constants.

`src/melee/if/ifstatus.h` was added with HUD-related types including `HudFlags`, `HudValue`, `HudIndex`, `Thing_803F9628`, placeholder return-value structs for `func_8016AE38`/`func_8016AE50`, function prototypes, and `extern HudIndex lbl_804A10C8`.
`obj_files.mk` stopped building `asm/melee/if/ifstatus.s.o` and instead builds `asm/melee/if/ifstatus_data.s.o` plus `src/melee/if/ifstatus.c.o`.
smart_moves: Kept unmigrated data in a dedicated assembly data file instead of blocking C progress on full data migration.
Used a mixed C plus inline-asm file to allow incremental decompilation while preserving binary behavior for functions not yet fully matched.
Added a header with provisional but useful struct layout knowledge, making subsequent `ifstatus` work less dependent on raw offsets.

Used descriptive field names where behavior was evident, such as `damage_percent`, `old_damage`, `frames_of_shake_remaining`, digit translation fields, digit velocity fields, and digit `HSD_JObj*` fields.
Explicitly labeled uncertain structures as placeholders and documented replacement intent, reducing the risk that temporary names would be mistaken for final domain knowledge.
Maintained an asm fallback for the only `NON_MATCHING` completed function, keeping the build match-safe while preserving the attempted C reconstruction.
decomp_lessons: Large interface/HUD files can be landed incrementally by separating code progress from data migration; this PR explicitly left data in assembly.
When converting raw assembly to a C file, keep unresolved functions as inline asm with original labels so nearby matched C can coexist with untouched code.
Headers for partially understood modules should capture known offsets and types, but should mark uncertain types and fields clearly with `unk`, `Placeholder`, comments, or TODOs.

For HUD-like data structures, naming fields by visible behavior, such as damage digits, translations, velocities, and shake frames, provides practical context even before all surrounding functions are named.
Build-list changes are essential when splitting one assembly object into a C object plus a separate data object; otherwise section/order assumptions can break.
A nonmatching C attempt is still useful if isolated behind `#ifdef NON_MATCHING` with a matching asm fallback and enough breadcrumbs, such as a decomp.me link, for future work.
naming_conventions: Known or semi-known HUD structs used PascalCase typedefs with leading-underscore struct tags, e.g. `typedef struct _HudFlags { ... } HudFlags;`, `HudValue`, and `HudIndex`.
Unresolved data symbols retained `lbl_80xxxxxx` names, e.g. `lbl_804A10C8`, `lbl_803F9628`, and `lbl_804DDA48`.
Unknown fields were named with `unk` plus offset or meaning hints, e.g. `unk258`, `unk26C`, `unk24CA`, while better-understood fields received descriptive names.
Temporary cross-module return types were explicitly named `Placeholder_..._ret_val` and included comments saying to replace them when the real function/type is understood.
HUD bitfields used behavior-oriented names when inferred, e.g. `explode_animation`, `force_digit_shake`, `hide_all_digits`, and `animation_status_id`, mixed with `unk` bits where evidence was weak.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used CodeWarrior inline asm functions with `asm return_type func(...)`, `nofralloc`, and original instruction comments to preserve assembly in a C translation unit.
Wrapped inline asm regions in `#pragma push`/`#pragma pop` and `// clang-format off/on` comments.
Adjusted small-data references for inline asm syntax, changing forms such as `lbl_804DDA74@sda21(r2)` to `lbl_804DDA74(r2)` and similar `r13`/`r2` references.
Preserved local labels such as `lbl_802F496C`, `lbl_802F49CC`, and `lbl_802F4B60` inside inline asm blocks.
Provided a C `NON_MATCHING` version of `func_802F6194` with a matching asm fallback, allowing future iterative matching without sacrificing the default build.
Kept constants and strings in `ifstatus_data.s`, including HUD model names like `DmgNum_scene_models`, `DmgMrk_scene_models`, and `ScInfCnt_scene_models`.

review_feedback.interpretation: The only recorded human feedback is terse; it indicates the PR consolidated progress into a single C source file, but there are no review comments explaining further rationale.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that migrate `asm/melee/if/ifstatus_data.s` data into C.
Find later PRs that remove `NON_MATCHING` from `func_802F6194`.
Find follow-up work renaming `HudIndex`, `HudValue`, or `Thing_803F9628` fields.
Search for later replacements of `Placeholder_8016AE38_ret_val` and `Placeholder_8016AE50_ret_val` with real player or HUD types.
Search later `ifstatus.c` changes for conversion of inline asm functions to matched C.
